finding: Terms & Conditions modal contains Lorem Ipsum placeholder text. Users are asked to click 'Agree' to accept nonsensical Latin text for a regulated financial product.
novice_impact: User may click Agree without reading, unknowingly consenting to undefined terms. If they do read it, trust is instantly destroyed.
seasoned_impact: Immediately signals the product is not production-ready. A seasoned investor will refuse to proceed.
recommendation: Replace Lorem Ipsum with actual DCI Terms and Conditions covering: product risks, settlement mechanics, early termination policy, regulatory disclaimers, and dispute resolution.

finding: No explicit risk warning or risk acknowledgment anywhere in the investment placement flow (Steps 1-4). The only risk information is buried in an FAQ accordion on the landing page. Users can commit $200,000 USD without ever being shown that they may receive a different currency at maturity.
novice_impact: User has no idea they could receive SGD instead of USD at maturity. May invest believing returns are guaranteed in their base currency.
seasoned_impact: Expects risk disclosures as standard practice. Absence may cause regulatory concern.
recommendation: Add a dedicated risk disclosure panel on the Review screen (before Submit) with: (1) explicit statement 'You may receive the Alternate Currency if the fixing rate is below the strike price at expiry', (2) a mandatory risk acknowledgment checkbox, (3) link to Key Fact Sheet/term sheet.

finding: Scenario B (Alternate Currency settlement) does not show the equivalent value in the base currency. User sees '257,000.00 SGD' but cannot tell if this represents a gain or loss compared to their 200,000 USD investment. The actual USD-equivalent loss is hidden.
novice_impact: Cannot assess risk. Sees two positive-looking scenarios with green/orange badges and may believe both are profitable outcomes.
seasoned_impact: Can mentally calculate the cross-rate but would prefer an explicit comparison.
recommendation: Add a line to Scenario B showing the base-currency equivalent: 'Equivalent to ~196,923 USD (based on current spot rate)' with red text if it represents a loss. Also add a clear label: 'This scenario means you receive SGD instead of your original USD.'

screen: All screens with forms and text
finding: 9 distinct WCAG AA contrast failures identified: helper/description text (~2.8:1), 'Expiring Soon' badge (~3.0:1), table column headers (~2.8:1), 'indicative yield'/'from spot' labels (~2.8:1), form placeholders (~2.3:1), footer text (~2.8:1), timestamp text (~2.3:1), 'Alternate Currency' badge (~4.0:1). Required: 4.5:1 for normal text.
novice_impact: Critical text that explains product mechanics is hardest to read. Users with visual impairments cannot use the product.
seasoned_impact: Same accessibility barriers apply regardless of expertise level.
recommendation: Increase all secondary text to minimum #595959 on white backgrounds (4.5:1). Status badges should use darker background colors or larger text. Conduct a full color audit using a contrast checker tool.

finding: Zero keyboard focus indicators are designed across all 29 screens. No focus rings on buttons, inputs, dropdowns, radio buttons, links, tabs, or modals. WCAG 2.4.7 (Focus Visible) is completely unmet.
novice_impact: Keyboard-only users cannot navigate the investment flow at all.
seasoned_impact: Same — accessibility failure affects all users equally.
recommendation: Add a 2px offset focus ring (e.g., blue #2563EB) to all interactive elements. Define focus order for the multi-step flow. Ensure the T&C modal traps focus correctly.

finding: After 'Strike Price Not Met' settlement (user received SGD instead of USD), the reinvestment flow silently swaps the currency pair from USD/SGD to SGD/EUR with no explanation. The investment amount changes from USD to SGD. User may not understand why the base currency changed.
novice_impact: Deeply confusing. User started with USD, received SGD, and is now being asked to invest SGD into EUR. No context is provided for this chain.
seasoned_impact: Understands the mechanics but would expect a clear explanation of the currency conversion and an option to convert back to USD first.
recommendation: Add an explanatory banner at the top of the reinvest screen: 'Your previous investment settled in SGD (Alternate Currency). You are now reinvesting your SGD proceeds.' Offer an option to convert back to the original base currency before reinvesting.

finding: Three different navigation bar configurations appear across the DCI flow: (A) 'FX and Investment' during placement, (B) 'Trade finance' on the MCA prerequisite screen with different casing, (C) 'FX and Treasury' on investment detail screens. Users cannot form a consistent mental model of where they are.
novice_impact: Disoriented — cannot tell if they are in the same product area. May think they have navigated to a different section.
seasoned_impact: Minor annoyance but still signals inconsistency and reduces trust in the platform.
recommendation: Standardise on one navigation label (recommend 'FX and Investment') across ALL DCI-related screens. Ensure consistent casing and active-state styling.

finding: Yield is displayed as '6.50% p.a.' (per annum) on a 1-month investment, but the actual return for the period is only ~0.54%. No clarification is provided. The annualized figure is visually prominent (large, bold) while no actual-period return is shown.
novice_impact: User likely interprets 6.50% as total return, not annualized. May invest expecting to earn $13,000 on $200,000 when actual return is ~$1,083.
seasoned_impact: Understands p.a. notation but would appreciate seeing both annualized and actual period return.
recommendation: Show both values: '6.50% p.a. (~0.54% for 1 month)'. Add a tooltip or info icon explaining 'Annual yield is prorated for your selected tenor. Your estimated return for this period is $1,083.33 USD.'

finding: No step indicator or progress bar in the multi-step DCI placement flow (Parameters → Accounts → Review → Confirmation). User has no idea how many steps remain or where they are in the process.
novice_impact: Increases anxiety in an already complex flow. User may abandon not knowing if there are 2 or 10 more steps.
seasoned_impact: Mildly frustrating — wants to know time commitment before starting.
recommendation: Add a horizontal stepper/progress indicator at the top of each step: 'Step 1 of 4: Investment Parameters → Step 2: Account Selection → Step 3: Review → Step 4: Confirmation'.

finding: Strike price selection offers 5 options (e.g., 1.2830 to 1.2870) with '% from spot' and 'indicative yield' but no explanation of what strike price means or how to choose. A novice sees radio buttons with decimal numbers and percentages with zero guidance.
novice_impact: Cannot make an informed decision. Will either pick randomly, pick the highest yield (without understanding the trade-off), or abandon.
seasoned_impact: Comfortable choosing but would appreciate seeing the full payoff profile.
recommendation: Add: (1) An info icon with tooltip explaining 'Strike price is the exchange rate that determines which currency you receive. Higher strike = higher yield but higher chance of receiving the alternate currency.' (2) A visual indicator showing spot rate position relative to strike options. (3) Label the trade-off: 'Higher yield ↔ Higher conversion risk'.

finding: Currency defaults to 'NIL' with no flag icon instead of a sensible default based on the user's account currencies. The 'Base Currency' and 'Alternate Currency' dropdowns show generic labels with no explanation of which is deposit vs. settlement currency.
novice_impact: Does not understand the difference between Base and Alternate currency. 'NIL' default is confusing — is it a currency code?
seasoned_impact: Minor — knows the terminology but a smart default would save time.
recommendation: Default to the user's primary account currency (e.g., USD). Add helper text under the currency pair: 'Base Currency = the currency you deposit. Alternate Currency = the currency you may receive at maturity.'

finding: The minimum amount helper text shows '$0,000.00 USD' which appears to be a formatting/template bug. Should display the actual minimum (e.g., '$50,000.00 USD').
novice_impact: Confusing — looks like a system error. Undermines trust.
seasoned_impact: Recognises it as a placeholder bug but still unprofessional.
recommendation: Fix the template to display the actual minimum amount for the selected currency. Format consistently with thousand separators.

finding: Green is overloaded across 6 different semantic meanings: active investment status, positive yield values, success checkmark, base currency scenario badge, strike price met indicator, and yield percentages. Users cannot quickly scan for meaning when the same colour means different things.
novice_impact: Sees green everywhere and assumes everything is positive/good, including Scenario A which may not be the preferred outcome.
seasoned_impact: Can parse from context but it slows scanning.
recommendation: Differentiate semantic uses: keep green for success/positive only. Use blue for informational status (Active), use a distinct colour (e.g., teal) for base currency scenarios, and use a neutral colour for yield percentages.

finding: Form input borders (~#E0E0E0 on white) have an estimated contrast ratio of ~2.5:1, failing WCAG 1.4.11 (Non-text Contrast) which requires 3:1. Input fields are visually indistinct from the background.
novice_impact: May not immediately recognise which elements are interactive form fields.
seasoned_impact: Same visual accessibility issue.
recommendation: Darken input borders to at least #949494 (3:1 on white). Consider adding a subtle background fill (#F9F9F9) to inactive inputs.

finding: Zero error states and zero loading states are designed across the entire 29-screen flow. No designs for: insufficient balance, rate expiry during placement, network timeout, session expiry, or invalid input.
novice_impact: If something goes wrong during a $200,000 investment, the user has no guidance on what happened or how to recover.
seasoned_impact: Expects robust error handling for high-value transactions. Absence is a red flag.
recommendation: Design error states for: (1) insufficient balance, (2) rate/quote expiry (with refresh option), (3) session timeout, (4) network error, (5) form validation errors (inline). Add loading/skeleton states for rate fetching and order submission.

finding: The 'REVIEW' label is positioned in the far-left margin, visually disconnected from the main content. Combined with no step indicator, the user's current position in the flow is unclear. The label does not follow the same visual pattern as other page titles.
novice_impact: May not realise this is a review/confirmation step before final submission.
seasoned_impact: Minor visual confusion but can infer from the Submit button.
recommendation: Replace with a proper step indicator showing 'Step 3 of 4: Review Your Investment'. Position as a page heading within the main content area.

finding: Status badges are inconsistent: 'Active' uses text-only styling, 'Expiring Soon' uses amber text, 'Awaiting Settlement' uses a pill badge with background colour. No unified design language for investment statuses.
novice_impact: Cannot quickly scan and understand investment statuses. The visual inconsistency reduces scanability.
seasoned_impact: Can parse from context but inconsistency slows portfolio review.
recommendation: Standardise all statuses as pill badges with consistent sizing: Active (green), Expiring Soon (amber), Awaiting Settlement (blue), Settled (gray). Use the same component for all.

finding: The 4 key DCI dates (Transaction, Value, Expiry, Maturity) use different labels across screens. The FAQ uses 'Trade Date' while the product flow uses 'Transaction Date'. The Investment Timeline explains each date but the explanation only appears in the parameters step, not on the investment details screen.
novice_impact: Confused by inconsistent terminology. May think 'Trade Date' and 'Transaction Date' are different things.
seasoned_impact: Minor — can map terms mentally.
recommendation: Standardise date labels across all screens and FAQs. Include the Investment Timeline explanation on the Investment Details screen as well, not just during placement.

finding: The confirmation screen shows only 'Place Another Investment' as the next action. There is no link to 'View My Portfolio', 'View Investment Details', or 'Return to Dashboard'. The user is stranded after placing a $200,000 investment.
novice_impact: Wants to verify their investment was recorded. No clear path to the portfolio.
seasoned_impact: Wants quick access to portfolio or the new investment's detail page.
recommendation: Add secondary actions: 'View Investment Details' (link to the new investment) and 'Go to Portfolio' (link back to the landing page). Keep 'Place Another Investment' as the secondary CTA.

finding: No inline validation is visible in any form screen. Users can only discover errors at submission time. The 'Next' button appears always enabled regardless of form completion state.
novice_impact: May attempt to proceed with incomplete fields and receive unexpected errors.
seasoned_impact: Expects real-time feedback when entering values outside valid ranges.
recommendation: Add inline validation for: minimum amount, valid currency pair combinations, tenor availability. Disable the Next button until required fields are completed. Show validation messages in real-time.

finding: The 'Expiring Soon' status shows 'Fixing Rate: Determined on 10 Nov 2025 (2PM, SGT)' but provides no countdown, no notification preference, and no action the user can take. The screen is purely informational with only a 'Back' button.
novice_impact: Does not know what to do while waiting. Would benefit from a notification option.
seasoned_impact: Understands the waiting period but would like a reminder/notification feature.
recommendation: Add: (1) a countdown to fixing date, (2) 'Set a reminder' option, (3) explanation of what happens after fixing. Consider showing the live spot rate vs strike price so users can estimate their likely outcome.

finding: The value proposition cards ('Maximise your investment returns' and 'Great for currency conversion needs') use small text and generic icons that do not visually differentiate from each other. Both look identical in weight and importance.
novice_impact: Does not quickly grasp the two distinct use cases for DCI.
seasoned_impact: Skips over marketing content — minimal impact.
recommendation: Use distinct, illustrative icons for each value prop. Increase heading font size. Consider adding a short 'How it works' visual (3-step diagram) that shows the DCI lifecycle: Invest → Wait for Expiry → Receive Settlement.
